NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David the servant of the LORD. An oracle within my heart concerning the transgression of the wicked: There is no fear of God before his eyes.
NKJV.2: For he flatters himself in his own eyes, When he finds out his iniquity and when he hates.
NKJV.3: The words of his mouth are wickedness and deceit; He has ceased to be wise and to do good.
NKJV.4: He devises wickedness on his bed; He sets himself in a way that is not good; He does not abhor evil.
NKJV.5: Your mercy, O LORD, is in the heavens; Your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.
NKJV.6: Your righteousness is like the great mountains; Your judgments are a great deep; O LORD, You preserve man and beast.
NKJV.7: How precious is Your lovingkindness, O God! Therefore the children of men put their trust under the shadow of Your wings.
NKJV.8: They are abundantly satisfied with the fullness of Your house, And You give them drink from the river of Your pleasures.
NKJV.9: For with You is the fountain of life; In Your light we see light.
NKJV.10: Oh, continue Your lovingkindness to those who know You, And Your righteousness to the upright in heart.
NKJV.11: Let not the foot of pride come against me, And let not the hand of the wicked drive me away.
NKJV.12: There the workers of iniquity have fallen; They have been cast down and are not able to rise.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm of David<S>1732</S> the servant<S>5650</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>. The transgression<S>6588</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S> saith<S>5002</S> within<S>7130</S> my heart<S>3820</S>, that there is no fear<S>6343</S> of God<S>430</S> before his eyes<S>5869</S>.
KJV.2: For he flattereth<S>2505</S> himself in his own eyes<S>5869</S>, until his iniquity<S>5771</S> be found<S>4672</S> to be hateful<S>8130</S>. <sup>until: Heb. to find his iniquity to hate</sup>
KJV.3: The words<S>1697</S> of his mouth<S>6310</S> are iniquity<S>205</S> and deceit<S>4820</S>: he hath left off<S>2308</S> to be wise<S>7919</S>, and to do good<S>3190</S>.
KJV.4: He deviseth<S>2803</S> mischief<S>205</S> upon his bed<S>4904</S>; he setteth<S>3320</S> himself in a way<S>1870</S> that is not good<S>2896</S>; he abhorreth<S>3988</S> not evil<S>7451</S>. <sup>mischief: or, vanity</sup>
KJV.5: Thy mercy<S>2617</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, is in the heavens<S>8064</S>; and thy faithfulness<S>530</S> reacheth unto the clouds<S>7834</S>.
KJV.6: Thy righteousness<S>6666</S> is like the great<S>410</S> mountains<S>2042</S>; thy judgments<S>4941</S> are a great<S>7227</S> deep<S>8415</S>: O LORD<S>3068</S>, thou preservest<S>3467</S> man<S>120</S> and beast<S>929</S>. <sup>the: Heb. the mountains of God</sup>
KJV.7: How excellent<S>3368</S> is thy lovingkindness<S>2617</S>, O God<S>430</S>! therefore the children<S>1121</S> of men<S>120</S> put their trust<S>2620</S> under the shadow<S>6738</S> of thy wings<S>3671</S>. <sup>excellent: Heb. precious</sup>
KJV.8: They shall be abundantly satisfied<S>7301</S> with the fatness<S>1880</S> of thy house<S>1004</S>; and thou shalt make them drink<S>8248</S> of the river<S>5158</S> of thy pleasures<S>5730</S>. <sup>abundantly: Heb. watered</sup>
KJV.9: For with thee is the fountain<S>4726</S> of life<S>2416</S>: in thy light<S>216</S> shall we see<S>7200</S> light<S>216</S>.
KJV.10: O continue<S>4900</S> thy lovingkindness<S>2617</S> unto them that know<S>3045</S> thee; and thy righteousness<S>6666</S> to the upright<S>3477</S> in heart<S>3820</S>. <sup>continue: Heb. draw out at length</sup>
KJV.11: Let not the foot<S>7272</S> of pride<S>1346</S> come<S>935</S> against me, and let not the hand<S>3027</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S> remove<S>5110</S> me.
KJV.12: There are the workers<S>6466</S> of iniquity<S>205</S> fallen<S>5307</S>: they are cast down<S>1760</S>, and shall not be able<S>3201</S> to rise<S>6965</S>.

ESV.1: Transgression speaks to the wicked deep in his heart;  there is no fear of God before his eyes.
ESV.2: For he flatters himself in his own eyes that his iniquity cannot be found out and hated.
ESV.3: The words of his mouth are trouble and deceit; he has ceased to act wisely and do good.
ESV.4: He plots trouble while on his bed; he sets himself in a way that is not good; he does not reject evil.
ESV.5: Your steadfast love, O LORD, extends to the heavens, your faithfulness to the clouds.
ESV.6: Your righteousness is like the mountains of God; your judgments are like the great deep; man and beast you save, O LORD.
ESV.7: How precious is your steadfast love, O God! The children of mankind take refuge in the shadow of your wings.
ESV.8: They feast on the abundance of your house, and you give them drink from the river of your delights.
ESV.9: For with you is the fountain of life; in your light do we see light.
ESV.10: Oh, continue your steadfast love to those who know you, and your righteousness to the upright of heart!
ESV.11: Let not the foot of arrogance come upon me, nor the hand of the wicked drive me away.
ESV.12: There the evildoers lie fallen; they are thrust down, unable to rise.

NASB.1: For the choir director. [A Psalm] of David the servant of the LORD. Transgression speaks to the ungodly within his heart; There is no fear of God before his eyes.
NASB.2: For it flatters him in his [own] eyes Concerning the discovery of his iniquity [and] the hatred [of it].
NASB.3: The words of his mouth are wickedness and deceit; He has ceased to be wise [and] to do good.
NASB.4: He plans wickedness upon his bed; He sets himself on a path that is not good; He does not despise evil.
NASB.5: Your lovingkindness, O LORD, extends to the heavens, Your faithfulness [reaches] to the skies.
NASB.6: Your righteousness is like the mountains of God; Your judgments are [like] a great deep. O LORD, You preserve man and beast.
NASB.7: How precious is Your lovingkindness, O God! And the children of men take refuge in the shadow of Your wings.
NASB.8: They drink their fill of the abundance of Your house; And You give them to drink of the river of Your delights.
NASB.9: For with You is the fountain of life; In Your light we see light.
NASB.10: O continue Your lovingkindness to those who know You, And Your righteousness to the upright in heart.
NASB.11: Let not the foot of pride come upon me, And let not the hand of the wicked drive me away.
NASB.12: There the doers of iniquity have fallen; They have been thrust down and cannot rise.

NLT.1:
Sin whispers to the wicked, deep within their hearts.
They have no fear of God at all.
NLT.2:
In their blind conceit,
they cannot see how wicked they really are.
NLT.3:
Everything they say is crooked and deceitful.
They refuse to act wisely or do good.
NLT.4:
They lie awake at night, hatching sinful plots.
Their actions are never good.
They make no attempt to turn from evil.
NLT.5:
Your unfailing love, O LORD, is as vast as the heavens;
your faithfulness reaches beyond the clouds.
NLT.6:
Your righteousness is like the mighty mountains,
your justice like the ocean depths.
You care for people and animals alike, O LORD.
NLT.7:
How precious is your unfailing love, O God!
All humanity finds shelter
in the shadow of your wings.
NLT.8:
You feed them from the abundance of your own house,
letting them drink from your river of delights.
NLT.9:
For you are the fountain of life,
the light by which we see.
NLT.10:
Pour out your unfailing love on those who love you;
give justice to those with honest hearts.
NLT.11:
Don’t let the proud trample me
or the wicked push me around.
NLT.12:
Look! Those who do evil have fallen!
They are thrown down, never to rise again.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. By David, the servant of Yahweh. An inner sanctuary is within my heart about the disobedience of the wicked: “There is no fear of God before his eyes.”
WEB.2: For he flatters himself in his own eyes, too much to detect and hate his sin.
WEB.3: The words of his mouth are iniquity and deceit. He has ceased to be wise and to do good.
WEB.4: He plots iniquity on his bed. He sets himself in a way that is not good. He doesn’t abhor evil.
WEB.5: Your loving kindness, Yahweh, is in the heavens. Your faithfulness reaches to the skies.
WEB.6: Your righteousness is like the mountains of God. Your judgments are like a great deep. Yahweh, you preserve man and animal.
WEB.7: How precious is your loving kindness, God! The children of men take refuge under the shadow of your wings.
WEB.8: They shall be abundantly satisfied with the abundance of your house. You will make them drink of the river of your pleasures.
WEB.9: For with you is the spring of life. In your light shall we see light.
WEB.10: Oh continue your loving kindness to those who know you, your righteousness to the upright in heart.
WEB.11: Don’t let the foot of pride come against me. Don’t let the hand of the wicked drive me away.
WEB.12: There the workers of iniquity are fallen. They are thrust down, and shall not be able to rise.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. Of David, the LORD’s servant. [1] An oracle within my heart
concerning the transgression of the wicked person:
Dread of God has no effect on him. [1] ⓓ
CSB17.2:
For with his flattering opinion of himself,
he does not discover and hate his iniquity. ⓔ
CSB17.3:
The words from his mouth are malicious and deceptive; ⓕ
he has stopped acting wisely and doing good. ⓖ
CSB17.4:
Even on his bed he makes malicious plans. ⓗ
He sets himself on a path that is not good,
and he does not reject evil. ⓘ
CSB17.5:
LORD, your faithful love reaches to heaven,
your faithfulness to the clouds. ⓙ
CSB17.6:
Your righteousness is like the highest mountains,
your judgments like the deepest sea. ⓚ
LORD, you preserve people and animals. ⓛ
CSB17.7:
How priceless your faithful love is, God!
People take refuge in the shadow of your wings. ⓜ
CSB17.8:
They are filled from the abundance of your house.
You let them drink from your refreshing stream. ⓝ
CSB17.9:
For the wellspring of life is with you. ⓐ
By means of your light we see light. ⓑ
CSB17.10:
Spread your faithful love over those who know you,
and your righteousness over the upright in heart. ⓒ
CSB17.11:
Do not let the foot of the arrogant come near me
or the hand of the wicked drive me away. ⓓ
CSB17.12:
There! The evildoers have fallen.
They have been thrown down and cannot rise. ⓔ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David the servant of the LORD. Transgression speaks [like an oracle] to the wicked (godless) [deep] within his heart;
There is no fear (dread) of God before his eyes.
AMP.2:
For he flatters and deceives himself in his own eyes
Thinking that his sinfulness will not be discovered and hated [by God].
AMP.3:
The words of his mouth are wicked and deceitful;
He has ceased to be wise and to do good.
AMP.4:
He plans wrongdoing on his bed;
He sets himself on a path that is not good;
He does not reject or despise evil.
AMP.5:
Your lovingkindness and graciousness, O LORD, extend to the skies,
Your faithfulness [reaches] to the clouds.
AMP.6:
Your righteousness is like the mountains of God,
Your judgments are like the great deep.
O LORD, You preserve man and beast.
AMP.7:
How precious is Your lovingkindness, O God!
The children of men take refuge in the shadow of Your wings.
AMP.8:
They drink their fill of the abundance of Your house;
And You allow them to drink from the river of Your delights.
AMP.9:
For with You is the fountain of life [the fountain of life-giving water];
In Your light we see light.
AMP.10:
O continue Your lovingkindness to those who know You,
And Your righteousness (salvation) to the upright in heart.
AMP.11:
Do not let the foot of the proud [person] overtake me,
And do not let the hand of the wicked drive me away.
AMP.12:
There those who [are perverse and] do evil have fallen;
They have been thrust down and cannot rise.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
The God-rebel tunes in to sedition—
all ears, eager to sin.
He has no regard for God,
he stands insolent before him.
MSG.2:
He has smooth-talked himself
into believing
That his evil
will never be noticed.
MSG.3:
Words gutter from his mouth,
dishwater dirty.
Can't remember when he
did anything decent.
MSG.4:
Every time he goes to bed,
he fathers another evil plot.
When he's loose on the streets,
nobody's safe.
He plays with fire
and doesn't care who gets burned.
MSG.5:
God's love is meteoric,
his loyalty astronomic,
MSG.6:
His purpose titanic,
his verdicts oceanic.
Yet in his largeness
nothing gets lost;
Not a man, not a mouse,
slips through the cracks.
MSG.7:
How exquisite your love, O God!
How eager we are to run under your wings,
MSG.8:
To eat our fill at the banquet you spread
as you fill our tankards with Eden spring water.
MSG.9:
You're a fountain of cascading light,
and you open our eyes to light.
MSG.10:
Keep on loving your friends;
do your work in welcoming hearts.
MSG.11:
Don't let the bullies kick me around,
the moral midgets slap me down.
MSG.12:
Send the upstarts sprawling
flat on their faces in the mud.

NIV.1:
Psalm 36
For the director of music. Of David the servant of the Lord.
An oracle is within my heart
concerning the sinfulness of the wicked:
There is no fear of God
before his eyes.
NIV.2:
For in his own eyes he flatters himself
too much to detect or hate his sin.
NIV.3:
The words of his mouth are wicked and deceitful;
he has ceased to be wise and to do good.
NIV.4:
Even on his bed he plots evil;
he commits himself to a sinful course
and does not reject what is wrong.
NIV.5:
Your love, O Lord, reaches to the heavens,
your faithfulness to the skies.
NIV.6:
Your righteousness is like the mighty mountains,
your justice like the great deep.
O Lord, you preserve both man and beast.
NIV.7:
How priceless is your unfailing love!
Both high and low among men
find refuge in the shadow of your wings.
NIV.8:
They feast on the abundance of your house;
you give them drink from your river of delights.
NIV.9:
For with you is the fountain of life;
in your light we see light.
NIV.10:
Continue your love to those who know you,
your righteousness to the upright in heart.
NIV.11:
May the foot of the proud not come against me,
nor the hand of the wicked drive me away.
NIV.12:
See how the evildoers lie fallen—
thrown down, not able to rise!

NRSVCE.1:
Transgression speaks to the wicked
deep in their hearts;
there is no fear of God
before their eyes.
NRSVCE.2:
For they flatter themselves in their own eyes
that their iniquity cannot be found out and hated.
NRSVCE.3:
The words of their mouths are mischief and deceit;
they have ceased to act wisely and do good.
NRSVCE.4:
They plot mischief while on their beds;
they are set on a way that is not good;
they do not reject evil.
NRSVCE.5:
Your steadfast love, O LORD, extends to the heavens,
your faithfulness to the clouds.
NRSVCE.6:
Your righteousness is like the mighty mountains,
your judgments are like the great deep;
you save humans and animals alike, O LORD.
NRSVCE.7:
How precious is your steadfast love, O God!
All people may take refuge in the shadow of your wings.
NRSVCE.8:
They feast on the abundance of your house,
and you give them drink from the river of your delights.
NRSVCE.9:
For with you is the fountain of life;
in your light we see light.
NRSVCE.10:
O continue your steadfast love to those who know you,
and your salvation to the upright of heart!
NRSVCE.11:
Do not let the foot of the arrogant tread on me,
or the hand of the wicked drive me away.
NRSVCE.12:
There the evildoers lie prostrate;
they are thrust down, unable to rise.

NET.1: An evil man is rebellious to the core. He does not fear God,
NET.2: for he is too proud to recognize and give up his sin.
NET.3: The words he speaks are sinful and deceitful; he does not care about doing what is wise and right.
NET.4: He plans ways to sin while he lies in bed; he is committed to a sinful lifestyle; he does not reject what is evil.
NET.5: O LORD, your loyal love reaches to the sky; your faithfulness to the clouds.
NET.6: Your justice is like the highest mountains, your fairness like the deepest sea; you preserve mankind and the animal kingdom.
NET.7: How precious is your loyal love, O God! The human race finds shelter under your wings.
NET.8: They are filled with food from your house, and you allow them to drink from the river of your delicacies.
NET.9: For you are the one who gives and sustains life.
NET.10: Extend your loyal love to your faithful followers, and vindicate the morally upright!
NET.11: Do not let arrogant men overtake me, or let evil men make me homeless!
NET.12: I can see the evildoers! They have fallen! They have been knocked down and are unable to get up!
